element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Learned and saved via password system during tournament

typical_tension_triggers: Facing stronger opponents with new movesets
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament ladder structure with escalating difficulty; gameplay criticized as sluggish in original version, improved in later ports

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden playable characters (Karate Croc, Dali Llama) accessible via secret button sequences

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial matches with basic movesets; introduction to attack strength mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tournament progression requiring special move acquisition and mastery; opponent diversity increases (various martial arts styles)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Challenge match against Dali Llama (series final boss); requires mastery of learned special moves and character-specific techniques
notable_spikes: Transition from basic attacks to special move requirements
Final boss match against Dali Llama

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear arcade-style ladder where player faces sequential opponents, each with distinct martial arts styles (Kung-Fu, Thai Boxing, Soviet Commando, etc.). Victory enables learning of new special moves between rounds.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Brutal Island Tournament structure across all versions

notable_level_patterns.description: Players earn and learn new attacks during tournament progression, saved via password system. This creates incentive to replay and experiment with movesets, extending engagement across tournament runs.
Brutal Unleashed sequel concept: return to same island four years later for another tournament, emphasizing cyclical tournament structure and character roster expansion with new fighters and arenas.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Brutal Unleashed: Above the Claw (sequel structure)
